title: Swift Structs vs Classes
content:
### Swift Structs vs Classes

Structs and classes are both building blocks for data in Swift, but they have key differences. Understanding when to use each is essential for effective Swift programming.

**Comparison Table:**

| Feature | Struct | Class |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Type | Value type | Reference type |
| Inheritance | No | Yes |
| Memberwise init | Automatic | Manual |
| Mutability | Must be `var` to modify | Mutable with `let` ref |
| Deinitializer | No | Yes |
| ARC | No (value semantics) | Yes |
| Identity (`===`) | No | Yes |

```swift
struct Point {
    var x: Double
    var y: Double
}

class Vehicle {
    var speed: Double = 0
    var make: String
    
    init(make: String) {
        self.make = make
    }
}

// Value vs reference semantics
var pointA = Point(x: 10, y: 20)
var pointB = pointA  // Copy
pointB.x = 30
print(pointA.x)  // 10 (unchanged)

var carA = Vehicle(make: "Tesla")
var carB = carA  // Same reference
carB.speed = 100
print(carA.speed)  // 100 (changed!)
```

**When to Use Struct:**

| Situation | Reason |
|-----------|--------|
| Small data values | Value semantics are safer |
| No inheritance needed | Structs are simpler |
| Immutable by default | `let` makes entire struct immutable |
| Thread safety | No shared mutable state |

**When to Use Class:**

| Situation | Reason |
|-----------|--------|
| Need inheritance | Class-only feature |
| Shared mutable state | Reference semantics needed |
| Objective-C interop | Some frameworks require classes |
| Deinitializer needed | Cleanup on deallocation |

> **Key Point:** Apple recommends using structs by default. Use classes only when you specifically need reference semantics or inheritance.

```swift
// Preferred: struct
struct User {
    var name: String
    var age: Int
}

// When needed: class
class DataManager {
    var cache: [String: Any] = [:]
    // Shared state across app
}
```

quiz.question: Which one has automatic memberwise initializer?
Which one does Apple recommend using by default?
